batch_metadata.timezone_note: Entirely EST (UTC-5). DST spring forward Mar 9, 2025 at 2:00 AM EST (07:00 UTC) is still ahead — will fall within B-024 range. RF-02 B-023 date_range.start states Feb 15; actual first message in this range is ROW-7762 on Feb 24 (Feb 15-23 is a real gap in the messaging record). RF-02 sms_secondary_section notes ROW-7763-7774 as secondary table entries — consistent with extracted range.
batch_metadata.note: Sparse cooperative batch. Feb 15-23 is a 9-day gap in the messaging record. Feb 24: payment + Olivia status update. Mar 3: Melissa requests Eli watch Olivia while she goes to hospital for suspected kidney stone — cooperative co-parenting. No RF-01 indicators.

ARC-RF01-001.notes: B-023 produces no RF-01 signals. 12 messages — all routine. Content: $270 payment (ROW-7762), Olivia status (ROW-7764-7765 'She's ok, watching videos'), cooperative childcare request around Melissa's hospital visit for suspected kidney stone/blood in urine (ROW-7766-7773). Eli responds supportively. Post-B-022 de-escalation pattern consistent with prior cycle.

batch_observations.tone_assessment: Entirely cooperative. Two date clusters: Feb 24 (ROW-7762-7765 — payment + Olivia update) and Mar 3 (ROW-7766-7773 — Melissa hospital request). Mar 3 is notably cooperative: Melissa reaches out asking Eli to take Olivia; Eli asks what's wrong and offers to help; Melissa discloses kidney stone symptoms (blood in urine). This is a moment of genuine co-parenting under a health emergency.
batch_observations.gap_period: Feb 15-23 is a 9-day gap — no messages after Feb 14 Valentine's Day batch through Feb 24. This represents the longest post-B-022-escalation quiet period in Phase B so far.
batch_observations.dst_note: DST spring forward occurs Mar 9, 2025 at 2:00 AM EST (clocks move to EDT, UTC-4). All B-023 messages predate the transition. B-024 will span the DST change — threshold is Mar 9, 2025 07:00 UTC.
